_comment: Storyboard for avatar-reel-composer. The agent WRITES this by reading the avatar's <video>.analysis.json and mirroring its rhythm. PACING (see SKILL.md): open with a ~2-3s hook, cut every ~3-6s, never let a shot linger (split long sentences across scenes at commas/colons and change the framing/zoom), and sprinkle B-roll. A 30s reel usually wants ~6-8 scenes, not 3-4. HARD RULE: the concatenation of every scene.text (joined with single spaces) must equal `script` verbatim — it is narrated as ONE take, then cut per scene. Paths resolve relative to where you run compose_reel.py (use --base-dir).

voice._note: Narration is synthesized ONE sentence at a time (cached per sentence in narration_parts/, so a mispronounced segment can be re-rolled with compose_reel.py --reroll N without redoing the rest) and joined with a small silence gap (speech-2.8-hd degrades on long single takes). language_boost defaults to 'None' so the CLONED voice keeps its own accent — boosting a language (e.g. 'Spanish') can drag a neutral/Chilean clone toward another regional accent (e.g. Argentinian voseo). You MAY sprinkle MiniMax expressive interjections in `script` — (sighs), (exhales), (laughs softly), etc. — and manual pauses <#0.5#>; they are spoken by the TTS and ignored by alignment/captions.

script: ¿Cuántas veces hoy abriste su perfil esperando una señal que en el fondo sabes que no va a llegar? Cada vez que revisas su última conexión, no buscas información: buscas calmar la ansiedad de no poder soltar. El contacto cero no es un castigo para el otro; es el espacio que tu sistema nervioso necesita para volver a calmarse. Y sí, al principio el silencio duele, pero ese mismo silencio es lo primero que empieza a devolverte a ti.

scenes._note: HOOK ~2-3s, eye-level — like the reference scene #0 (2.97s). The first sentence is split here and continues over B-roll in s2.

scenes.text: esperando una señal que en el fondo sabes que no va a llegar?
scenes.broll_description: primer plano de unas manos sosteniendo un smartphone en la penumbra de un dormitorio de noche, la cara apenas iluminada por la luz azul de la pantalla mostrando un chat
scenes.broll_action: los dedos se deslizan lenta y pausadamente por la pantalla, la persona respira y cambia ligeramente de postura; movimientos pequeños, calmados y realistas
scenes._note: Cut to B-roll immediately after the hook (reference pattern #0 -> #1). B-roll already carries its camera move, so motion=none.

scenes.text: Cada vez que revisas su última conexión, no buscas información:
scenes.broll_description: primerísimo plano de la pantalla de un smartphone que muestra la hora de 'última conexión' de un chat y una foto de perfil difusa, el pulgar a punto de tocar, luz tenue nocturna, fondo desenfocado
scenes.broll_action: el pulgar se acerca despacio a la pantalla y duda sin llegar a tocar; micro-movimientos lentos, contenidos y realistas
scenes._note: Tighter second phone insert to keep the rhythm; this sentence is split at the colon, with the payoff returning to the presenter in s4.

scenes.text: buscas calmar la ansiedad de no poder soltar.
scenes._note: Presenter returns with an emphasis zoom-in (zoom_in).

scenes.text: El contacto cero no es un castigo para el otro;
scenes._note: Different angle + zoom-OUT so consecutive talking-heads read as distinct cuts (reference alternates zoom_in/zoom_out).

scenes.text: es el espacio que tu sistema nervioso necesita para volver a calmarse.
Y sí, al principio el silencio duele, pero ese mismo silencio es lo primero que empieza a devolverte a ti.
scenes.broll_description: una mujer pensativa de unos 40 años mira por la ventana de su casa al atardecer, luz cálida de contraluz, plano medio, atmósfera melancólica y serena
scenes.broll_action: respira hondo, parpadea lento y baja un poco la mirada; gestos mínimos, melancólicos y contenidos, sin hablar
scenes._note: Emotional B-roll outro — the one shot allowed to breathe a little longer (~7-8s).

finish._note: Finishing pass: serif phrase-unit captions that REPLACE each other (no stale stacking), with a bold-italic PAYOFF highlight on each breath group's completion — matching the analyzed reels — plus a FIXED-volume, light instrumental bed in the reel's emotional tone. Runs automatically (enabled=true) or via compose_reel.py --finish. TAILOR music_prompt to the script + B-roll (here: heartbreak / no-contact / healing -> tender, melancholic-but-hopeful piano). style_from points to a subtitle_style.json captured by avatar-frames from the ORIGINAL reels; it drives caption position/size/casing (font stays serif + bold-italic emphasis).
finish.music_prompt: sparse, intimate solo piano for an emotional reflection on heartbreak and letting go, tender and a little melancholic turning gently hopeful, very minimal and slow, soft sustained pad underneath, no drums, no beat, no vocals, quiet unobtrusive bed

fx._note: Polish pass (stage 4), applied OVER the finished video; keeps final-without-sfx.mp4. The transition look (golden/white flash, dip, or none; duration; strength; which boundary types) comes from the avatar's MEASURED transition_style.json — run profile_transitions.py once per avatar on its *.analysis.json files. Omit transition_style here to use that profile; set it only to override. Always duration-preserving — never a crossfade. SFX: soft whoosh leading each B-roll cut + soft low boom under emphasis scenes, sparse (~1/15s) and very quiet. guide = a voice-isolate voice.json of an ORIGINAL reel; its measured sfx_intervals set the density.
